Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple Water and wastewater systems are targeted by exploiting known vulnerabilities in internet-facing web applications to gain initial access, followed by uploading malicious payloads and creating new user accounts to escalate privileges and disrupt operations.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Expert-level attackers aiming to disrupt critical infrastructure operations.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shadow IT, Poor implementation of access controls, Authentication issues

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2021-30563 [CWE-502]: Apache Log4j2 JNDI features used in configuration...
stage.description: The threat actor exploited a vulnerability in an internet-facing web application using the Apache Log4j2 JNDI feature, allowing them to remotely execute arbitrary code and gain initial access to the targeted water and wastewater systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.kev: CVE-2024-8963 [CWE-918]: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
stage.description: The threat actor uploaded malicious payloads onto the compromised system by exploiting a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability, establishing persistence and executing further attacks.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The threat actor manipulated or disrupted SCADA systems through phishing campaigns targeting employees, causing operational disruptions and potential physical damage to infrastructure.

Scenario.summary: Expert-level threat actors exploited known vulnerabilities in internet-facing web applications to gain initial access to water and wastewater systems. They then established persistence by uploading malicious payloads and created new user accounts to escalate privileges. Finally, they disrupted operations by manipulating SCADA systems, leading to potential service outages and physical damage.
